point_reviews.reason: The document confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk.
External evidence supports that the virus can land on the face of a nearby person after a sneeze or cough, or be inhaled in areas with low airflow.
The document states that COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms.
External evidence supports that the risk of catching COVID-19 increases with close contact, poor airflow, and activities like talking or sharing a car.
External evidence supports that living with someone who has COVID-19 or spending time in crowded spaces increases the risk of infection.
The document confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected people.
The document confirms that COVID-19 can spread from touching contaminated surfaces and then touching the face.
The local document does not mention this point, and it is not supported by external evidence.

External evidence supports that COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing, or breathe.
External evidence supports that the virus can be transmitted via larger respiratory droplets and smaller aerosols.
External evidence supports that people can spread COVID-19 even if they are not showing symptoms.
External evidence supports that COVID-19 can spread by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contacting contaminated surfaces.
External evidence supports that research is ongoing to understand the most risky situations for COVID-19 spread.
